title: tetrarrhena oreophila var. minor
intro: Tetrarrhena oreophila var. minor, also known as the lesser mountain grass, is a type of plant species native to New Guinea. The leaves and stems of the lesser mountain grass have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids. Tetrarrhena oreophila var. minor can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
